greeting: Good morning, Jean. Today is Friday, July 24, and the operating shape is a tightly stacked meeting day: seven meetings plus one protected block, with Legal Change Management, EPMO EOW, Salesforce/program pipeline, and executive status updates as the main anchors. There are three high-priority threads to keep in front: prep the EOW/program updates, protect the afternoon handoffs across Salesforce and pipeline decisions, and triage the failed CoE Portal refresh. I could not safely read prior digest history from the current dashboard file, so no carry-over was generated; Teams and SharePoint context were rate-limited, so this brief relies on calendar, email, and current GitHub state.

items.label: 10:00 AM - Legal Change Management - IT & EPMO Sync
items.detail: Use this 30-minute sync to clear IT/EPMO doubts, action items, and clarifications before the later portfolio and status meetings.

items.label: 10:30 AM - IPP Q&A
items.detail: Back-to-back after Legal Change Management, so prep any questions or decisions before the 10:00 AM meeting starts.

items.label: 12:00 PM - Blocked Space
items.detail: This is the clearest protected block before the afternoon stack; use it for meeting prep or urgent follow-through.

items.label: 1:00 PM - EPMO EOW Check In
items.detail: The invite was moved one hour earlier and adds Nadia so programmatic updates can be included.

items.label: 2:00 PM - Salesforce Program Standup
items.detail: Review EPMO status, enterprise timeline, and RAID with focus on milestones, health, risks, dependencies, and decisions.

items.label: 2:30 PM - Project pipeline (EPMO/IT weekly sync)
items.detail: Prioritization, BA assignment, active-project shifts, and UAT status are the named decision areas.

items.label: 3:00 PM - Executive Project Status Slide Update
items.detail: Update the executive project deck after the Salesforce and pipeline discussions so the slide work reflects the latest risks and decisions.

items.label: 3:30 PM - 7/29 EPMO Meeting planning
items.detail: Lightweight planning block for the July 29 EPMO meeting; keep it bounded after a dense operational afternoon.

items.label: Prepare the EPMO EOW program readout
items.detail: The EOW check-in was moved earlier and expanded to include programmatic updates, so have the top updates, risks, and asks ready before 1:00 PM.

items.label: Use the afternoon stack to lock Salesforce and pipeline decisions
items.detail: Salesforce standup, project pipeline, and executive slide update run consecutively from 2:00 to 3:30 PM. Capture prioritization changes, BA assignment needs, UAT status, and anything that belongs in the executive deck.

items.label: Clear Legal Change Management IT/EPMO questions
items.detail: The morning sync is explicitly for doubts, action items, and clarifications. Bring the open questions into the 10:00 AM discussion so they do not spill into next week.

items.label: Triage the failed CoE Portal refresh
items.detail: GitHub reported that the EPMO Portal Asana refresh workflow failed this morning. Check the failed run before the portal data becomes stale for stakeholders.

items.label: Reply to Yash on the Latent Variables platform questions
items.detail: Yash offered to answer questions by email or set a 20-minute call, with availability today and tomorrow in Pacific time. Decide whether this should be handled asynchronously or scheduled with the Juniper/Latent Variables thread.

items.label: Jhara Ochoa is out of office
items.detail: The calendar shows Jhara OOO through today, which may affect Legal Change Management follow-through or any Jhara-owned next steps.

items.label: Monday starts with timecards and Legal Change Management
items.detail: Approve Timecards & Prepare for the Week is at 8:00 AM, followed by Legal Change Management at 10:00 AM.

items.label: Amazon Connect Venn UAT Phase II is the main readiness anchor
items.detail: The 11:30 AM session is scoped to environment readiness, Phase II test scenarios, tester access, phone numbers, configuration, schedule, and support process.

items.label: Afternoon portfolio reviews continue
items.detail: BD Portfolio Review and the Maria 1:1 both name roadblocks, portfolio status, and next steps as useful prep areas.
